Senecio peregrinus, also known as the "bushy fireweed," is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant health benefits, which can help in the treatment of various health conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Senecio peregrinus include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
